title: Albuterol: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:

What side effects can this medication cause? 
Albuterol may cause side effects. Tell your doctor if any of these symptoms are severe or do not go away: 
nervousness shakiness dizziness headache uncontrollable shaking of a part of the body muscle cramps excessive motion or activity sudden changes in mood nosebleed nausea increased or decreased appetite difficulty falling asleep or staying asleep pale skin Some side effects can be serious. If you experience any of these symptoms, call your doctor immediately: 
fast, pounding, or irregular heartbeat chest pain fever blisters or rash hives itching swelling of the face, throat, tongue, lips, eyes, hands, feet, ankles, or lower legs increased difficulty breathing difficulty swallowing hoarseness Albuterol may cause other side effects. Call your doctor if you have any unusual problems while taking this medication.
What should I know about storage and disposal of this medication? 
Keep this medication in the container it came in, tightly closed, and out of reach of children. Store it at room temperature and away from excess heat and moisture (not in the bathroom).
